title: Structured-output failure modes
description: Model JSON fails through truncation, extra prose, wrong types, missing required fields, hallucinated enums, and trailing-comma syntax errors.
body: Use when a structured-output path is flaky. Common defects: output truncated by max-tokens (length finish-reason); JSON wrapped in prose or code fences; correct shape but wrong types or coerced numbers; required fields omitted; enum values invented outside the allowed set. Do not paper over these by regex-extracting JSON and coercing, and avoid silent field drops. Actions: validate against a strict schema, detect the length finish-reason as truncation, and route failures into a bounded repair loop. Validation signals include a conformance run that classifies each failure type and tests that truncation and bad enums are rejected. Risk boundary: coercing a malformed payload to satisfy a type can corrupt downstream state; fail closed instead.
